name: develop-backend
description: Spring Boot/MyBatisによるバックエンド実装スキル - RESTful API設計、データベース設計（Flywayマイグレーション）、Controller/Service/Mapper層の実装、単体テスト作成を行います。DRY原則を徹底し、product.utilパッケージの既存実装やAOPによる自動ログ出力を活用します。未使用コード削除をIDE警告で確認し、./gradlew checkでLint/テスト/カバレッジ80%以上を保証します。サーバー起動による動作確認も必須です。
capabilities: Design and implement RESTful APIs with Spring Boot
Implement Controller, Service, and Mapper layers
Write JUnit 5 unit tests with Mockito
Run `./gradlew check` for lint and coverage validation
Verify server startup and API functionality
author: Crearize
securitySummary: The static analyzer flagged 72 potential issues but all are FALSE POSITIVES. This is a documentation/metadata file describing a Spring Boot development workflow. The 'external_commands' detections are markdown code blocks showing legitimate Gradle/git commands, not executable code. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'obfuscation' findings are references to JwtUtil class names and technical documentation. The skill-report.json in the same directory already correctly classified this as low risk with no blocking issues. All detected patterns are standard development documentation patterns, not security threats.
valueStatement: Creating backend APIs with Spring Boot requires following proper patterns, database migrations, and passing quality gates. This skill provides a complete development workflow with test coverage requirements and lint validation.
userTitle: Build Spring Boot backends with tests and quality checks
limitations: Focuses on Spring Boot/MyBatis stack only
Requires existing project structure with Gradle
Does not generate frontend code or deployment configurations

useCases.title: Implement new REST endpoints
useCases.description: Add new API endpoints following project conventions with proper layer separation and tests

useCases.title: Create database schemas
useCases.description: Design database tables with Flyway migrations and update related entity/DTO classes

useCases.title: Verify code quality
useCases.description: Run lint checks and ensure test coverage meets 80% threshold before merge

promptTemplates.title: Create simple CRUD API
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new REST API endpoint for [resource-name] with standard CRUD operations following the project patterns. Include Flyway migration, entity, DTO, mapper, service, and controller. Ensure 80% test coverage.

promptTemplates.title: Add validation rules
promptTemplates.prompt: Add input validation to the [existing-endpoint] API. Use Bean Validation annotations and return proper error responses. Update unit tests to cover validation scenarios.

promptTemplates.title: Write integration tests
promptTemplates.prompt: Create integration tests for the [api-name] endpoint. Test success cases, validation errors, and edge cases. Verify coverage meets the 80% threshold.

promptTemplates.title: Optimize database queries
promptTemplates.prompt: Review the [mapper-name] mapper for N+1 query issues or missing indexes. Optimize the SQL queries and add appropriate result maps. Verify changes with `./gradlew check`.

bestPractices: Follow existing patterns from similar implementations in the codebase
Use product.util package utilities instead of creating duplicates
Run `./gradlew check` and fix all issues before committing
antiPatterns: Skipping test coverage validation before merge
Writing try-catch blocks instead of using ExceptionHandler
Manually logging when AOP handles logging automatically

faq.question: Which Java frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill is optimized for Spring Boot with MyBatis. Other frameworks require manual adaptation of patterns.

faq.question: What test coverage threshold is enforced?
faq.answer: Minimum 80% line coverage is required. Business logic should achieve 90% coverage.

faq.question: Can this skill work with Maven instead of Gradle?
faq.answer: The skill references Gradle commands. Maven users need to translate `./gradlew check` and bootRun to equivalent Maven goals.

faq.question: Is my code safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only reads and writes within your project directory. No data is sent to external services.

faq.question: Why does the skill reference specific utility classes?
faq.answer: The skill references project-specific utilities like SecurityUtils and JwtUtil to encourage reuse and avoid duplicating existing functionality.

faq.question: How does this differ from generic code generation?
faq.answer: This skill follows your project specific conventions, coding rules, and existing utilities. It enforces quality gates and documentation updates.
